title: T-042 — production scanner output co-product waste APIs (06-e)
prompt:
# T-042 — production scanner output co-product waste APIs (06-e)

PRD: docs/prd/06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.5 FR-SC-BE-050, §8.5 FR-SC-BE-051, §8.5 FR-SC-BE-052
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement scanner-facing production output, co-product, and waste endpoints with LP genealogy and no-LP waste logging.

## Implementation contract
1. Add production scanner output, co-product, and waste handlers. 2. Validate WO in executable state with approved WO snapshot, active BOM, active factory spec, and site-line context. 3. Output and co-product create LPs and genealogy; waste creates wo_waste_log only. 4. Emit audit and outbox events for every write.

## Files
- app/api/production/scanner/output/route.ts [create]
- app/api/production/scanner/co-product/route.ts [create]
- app/api/production/scanner/waste/route.ts [create]
- lib/services/production/scanner-output.ts [create]
- tests/production/scanner-output.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a valid IN_PROGRESS WO with complete snapshot, when POST production scanner output runs, then a primary LP, wo_outputs row, genealogy link, audit log, and outbox event are created
2. Given a co-product registration, when product belongs to the BOM co_products contract, then the LP is created with type co_product and linked to the same WO genealogy
3. Given a waste registration, when category and qty are valid, then wo_waste_log is created and no LP row is created
4. Given WO snapshot lacks approved BOM or active factory spec, when any endpoint is called, then the API returns SC_WO_RELEASE_CONTRACT_BLOCKED

## Test strategy
- RED: write route and service tests for output, co-product, waste, and blocked WO snapshot before implementation
- Run pnpm test production scanner-output

## Risk red lines
- Do not let D365 metadata unlock scanner production writes
- Do not create LPs for waste
- Do not mutate consumed LP genealogy outside the production service transaction
